rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or declared as helpers in the same annex.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers that are not present in the model's configured dependencies.
Validate that all AGREE expressions have valid types; resolve 'named thing must be an expression with a type' errors by correcting root references.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all referenced identifiers are either declared in the same annex or are visible features of the component.
Use only scalar types int, real, and bool in AGREE constant and equation declarations.
Align helper declaration types with the types of their assigned expressions to prevent type mismatch errors.
Minimize repairs to only the elements causing validation failures, preserving unrelated architecture.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks within valid AADL component scopes.
Implementation-level bindings should be placed in component implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections are defined.
Property associations and references must match the model's configured dependencies and package structure.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new identifiers or types that are not declared or visible in the current AADL scope.
Do not assume that identifiers or types from component types are automatically available in implementation scopes.
Do not add architecture elements to resolve AGREE diagnostics unless they are explicitly required by the model.
irrelevant_topics: Component type annexes and implementation annexes
Property association and package reference rules
